claim: An 11,022-token excerpt of a medieval Latin herbal-encyclopedic text in PROSE form (Isidore of Seville, Etymologiae Book 17 'De rebus rusticis' — primary) reproduces all four paragraph-structure signature features of Hand A from H-BV-RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 and H-BV-HIGH-LOW-STRUCTURE-01 (header recurrence near Hand A's 4.6%, vocabulary disjunction near 85%, top-20 medial recipe-verb properties near 19/20, anti-alternating adjacency near 47%), within specified relative tolerance bands. A secondary VERSE corpus (Macer Floridus) runs alongside as a known-verse control to show which signature features are prose/verse-sensitive and which are genre-robust. Verdict combines the two: CONFIRMED requires Isidore match on all four; Macer is informational.
evidence_for: H-BV-RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 MARGINAL (2/3) resolved substantively to 'herbal-encyclopedic' — unique headers + reusable body + no header recurrence

Isidore of Seville's Etymologiae Book 17 is the canonical medieval Latin encyclopedic reference (compiled c.630, widely copied through 15th c.), with per-topic prose entries exactly matching the encyclopedic structure we are testing for
A dual-benchmark design (prose primary + verse secondary) guards against both threshold-calibration errors and genre-form confounds
evidence_against: Isidore's sections are subdivided [1], [2], ... within thematic chapters ('De agricultura', 'De frumentis', etc.) — unit sizes may differ from Hand A's gallows-delimited paragraphs (mean 40 tokens)
Unit-size mismatch may affect per-paragraph type/token behaviour and header recurrence statistics; relative thresholds mitigate but do not eliminate this

A pre-registered relative-match rule with +/-5pp tolerance is stricter than an abstract threshold and risks a false negative if Isidore's paragraph structure differs from Hand A by even modest amounts that are NOT due to cipher/code structure
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — AMENDED BEFORE EXECUTION.

  CORPORA (frozen at pre-registration):
    PRIMARY (prose): Isidore of Seville, Etymologiae Book 17 'De rebus rusticis', from thelatinlibrary.com/isidore/17.shtml, saved verbatim to `raw/corpus/reference-corpora/isidore_etym_17_raw.html`. ~10,362 word tokens — padded with Etymologiae Book 18 from the same source if under 11,022.
    SECONDARY (verse, sanity check): Macer Floridus De viribus herbarum (Choulant 1832 Leipzig edition) from archive.org/details/deviribusherbaru00mace, saved to `raw/corpus/reference-corpora/macer_floridus_raw.txt`. Known verse/metre caveat — reported but NOT binding on verdict.

  PARAGRAPH SEGMENTATION (locked per corpus):
    Hand A: 271 paragraphs from H-BV-HIGH-LOW-STRUCTURE-01 (boundary = line beginning with plain gallows t or p not followed by h).
    Isidore: boundaries at each numbered section marker [N] within the Etymologiae Book 17 text (standard editorial numbering preserved in the Latin Library copy). A paragraph = content between two such markers.
    Macer: boundaries at chapter heading lines (roman numeral + period + uppercase plant name, e.g. 'III. ABSINTHIUM.'). A paragraph = verse content between two such headings, with OCR apparatus lines filtered (Greek characters, digit-initial apparatus).

  CLEANING (locked):
    Isidore: strip HTML; lowercase; replace punctuation with spaces; keep only ASCII letters and whitespace; truncate to 11,022 tokens (padding with Book 18 if needed).
    Macer: restrict to range between the first 'III. ABSINTHIUM.' line and the first 'CONFECTBUCH' line; strip Greek-containing and digit-initial lines; lowercase; keep only ASCII letters; truncate to 11,022 tokens.
    Hand A: already tokenised in H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-01.

  MEASURES (identical for all three corpora):
    (M1) HIGH/LOW split: rank R at cumulative 50% tokens.
    (M2) Vocabulary disjunction: |INITIAL_types \ MIDDLE_types| / |INITIAL_types| with position bins INITIAL/FIRST/MIDDLE/FINAL computed per paragraph.
    (M3) Top-20 MIDDLE token recipe-verb passes: consonant-skeleton variants >=2 AND paragraph-level coverage >=30%.
    (M4) Header recurrence rate: fraction of distinct INITIAL-position types that initiate >=3 paragraphs.
    (M5) Cross-class adjacency rate HL+LH using the corpus's own HIGH set.

  RELATIVE-MATCH TOLERANCE BANDS (locked, Hand A values from prior tests):
    Hand A reference values: disjunction 0.850, top-20 medial 19/20, header recur 0.046, cross-class 0.469.
    Tolerance bands (absolute distance from Hand A):
      disjunction:       +/- 0.10  (match if in [0.75, 0.95])
      top-20 medial:     +/- 4     (match if in [15, 20])
      header recurrence: +/- 0.05  (match if in [0.00, 0.096])
      cross-class rate:  +/- 0.05  (match if in [0.419, 0.519])

  PARAGRAPH-SIZE UNIT NORMALISATION:
    Report mean paragraph length in tokens for each corpus. If Isidore's mean paragraph length is more than 2x or less than 0.5x Hand A's 40.7 tokens, add a unit-size caveat to the verdict — the paragraphs are not size-matched and any signature deviation may reflect unit scale rather than structural genre.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED (herbal-encyclopedic reframe supported) if PRIMARY Isidore matches Hand A on ALL FOUR locked tolerance bands (disjunction, top-20 medial, header recurrence, cross-class). SECONDARY Macer values reported and discussed but NOT binding on the verdict. REFUTED if Isidore shows header recurrence >= 0.30 (classical-recipe pattern) OR cross-class >= 0.55 (natural function/content alternation) — either is a direct contradiction of the herbal-encyclopedic reading. MARGINAL in any intermediate case (Isidore matches 2-3 of 4 bands with no refute conditions).
tests_remaining: Execute Isidore + Macer benchmark comparison in separate commit after lock
amendment_note: Before execution, amended to address three pre-execution concerns: (1) Macer is verse not prose, potentially confounding genre with structure — added Isidore Etymologiae Book 17 as primary PROSE benchmark; Macer relegated to secondary verse-control. (2) Absolute thresholds risked false confirm/refute against a benchmark whose properties were unknown — replaced with relative-match tolerance bands anchored to Hand A's observed values. (3) Paragraph segmentation defined per-corpus (Hand A gallows / Isidore section-numbering / Macer chapter-heading) with unit-size caveat. Commit of this amendment is the new lock point.
reasoning: The RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 result pointed at a herbal-encyclopedic structure. To test that reading rigorously, compare Hand A against a prose medieval Latin encyclopedia (Isidore Book 17), not just a verse didactic poem (Macer). The pre-execution review raised three concerns — verse/prose confound, absolute vs relative thresholds, paragraph-unit mismatch — all three addressed in this amendment.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-directed benchmark test; amended after three pre-execution review concerns
